feed.title: Combined summary - Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs

entry.title: Combined summary - Fountain Codes: a way to reduce blockchain storage costs
entry.summary: Fountain Codes are increasingly regarded as an innovative solution for enhancing the storage and management of Bitcoin's blockchain data. By segmenting the blockchain into numerous encoded parts known as droplets, this technique allows nodes to reconstruct the entire blockchain by obtaining a sufficient number of these droplets. This results in each node only needing to store a portion of the total encoded data, significantly reducing individual storage requirements. The research, conducted in collaboration with Vinteum, aims to use Fountain Codes to boost the efficiency and scalability of the Bitcoin network. The comprehensive findings and methodologies have been extensively discussed in a blog post available at [this link](https://lucasdbr05.com/posts/fountain-codes/). This exploration delves into both the potential benefits and the technical complexities of integrating advanced coding techniques with existing blockchain architectures. The utilization of deterministic droplet composition based on per-peer seed and block height is another aspect that has been explored to enhance blockchain storage strategies. This approach not only conserves data but also ensures a fair random selection of droplets, leading to substantial storage savings. For instance, using parameters such as $k=1,000$ and $s=10$, specific sets of droplets for predetermined blocks can be determined and constructed incrementally. This strategy could potentially reduce disk space usage significantly, making more copies of the blockchain available for download and potentially future-proofing the technology. On the other hand, the adoption of Reed-Solomon (RS) codes over fountain codes has been preferred due to security concerns posed by potentially malicious peers within the network. RS codes facilitate polynomial-time decoding, which is essential for correcting errors with contributions from a few reliable peers, thereby mitigating risks associated with malevolent participants. Additionally, RS codes help maintain anonymity when nodes switch network identities, which is crucial for preserving privacy and security within the network. These aspects influence various protocol decisions, including how data is advertised and distributed among nodes. More insights into the decoding speeds and practical applications of RS codes in the absence of malicious peers can be found at the [GitHub repository for leopard](https://github.com/catid/leopard/). In summary, while Fountain Codes offer a promising avenue for blockchain data management by reducing storage demands, RS codes present a more secure and robust alternative in environments where peer reliability cannot be guaranteed. The ongoing research and discussions continue to shape the strategies for optimal blockchain data synchronization and storage solutions, reflecting a dynamic engagement with technological advancements in the field.
